channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: Flavored Vapes Can Save Lives
item.description: Last week, the Food and Drug Administration quietly released the results of its annual youth tobacco survey, revealing a significant public health achievement. The data showed a dramatic decline in vaping among high school students, dropping from nearly 30 percent in 2019 to just 5.2 percent in 2025. Even more striking, only 1.4 percent of high school students reported smoking, meaning just over one percent still use traditional cigarettes. Despite this remarkable progress, which could be considered one of the greatest successes in public health history, organizations such as the Campaign for Tobacco-Free Kids, the American Lung Association, and the FDA are focusing their attention on the debate over whether flavored e-cigarettes should be permitted, rather than celebrating the overall decline in youth tobacco use. (Published on March 17, 2026bythefp.com)

item.title: Planned Denial | FDA’s Shifting Standards For Vape Authorization
item.description: The FDA's nicotine regulation in the US is criticized for hindering safer nicotine products while allowing harmful cigarettes. Dr. Ian Fearon explains the challenges in the regulatory system, including a high number of PMTA applications and fluctuating standards. The rise of illicit markets and a decline in youth vaping are also discussed. The FDA's failure to adapt is questioned. (Published on March 18, 2026byregulatorwatch.com)

item.title: Complementing and expanding Chris Snowdon's latest Substack on passive vaping
item.description: The text notes the weakness in evidence against risks of passive vaping. It criticizes public health studies for inaccurately associating vape aerosols with harmful particulates, emphasizing unrealistic protocols in generating aerosols. The study highlights flaws in self-reported data and unrealistic chamber studies. It mentions various flawed studies on exposure to vape aerosol in cars and suggests these assessments are alarmist and lack realism. (Published on March 18, 2026bysubstack.com)

item.title: Polish crackdown on new nicotine products praised by medical experts
item.description: Poland’s decision to tighten its tobacco control laws, banning disposable e-cigarettes and restricting nicotine pouches, has been strongly backed by leading medical societies, which warn that the products pose risks to public health. Producers affected by the new rules claim the measures are disproportionate and market-destroying. (Published on March 18, 2026byeuractiv.com)

item.title: Smokeless nicotine seen as ‘bridge’ for military members, veterans quitting cigarettes
item.description: Smokeless nicotine products are increasingly viewed as a transitional tool to help military members quit traditional cigarettes. Experts note that tobacco use is deeply ingrained in military culture, making cessation particularly challenging. Health professionals, including Stephen Ferrara, emphasize the severe long-term consequences of smoking, such as stroke and critical limb ischemia. They argue that moving away from combustible cigarettes can significantly reduce these health risks. Overall, harm reduction strategies are being promoted as a practical step toward improving the health of service members and veterans. (Published on March 18, 2026bythehill.com)
